tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58327,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Responses README missing x-agent-session-id header, internal activity source, and new observability features","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58314 (commit 54ef6f748) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58314 added several significant features and breaking changes to the AgentServer Responses library: - `x-agent-session-id` response header is now returned on **all** Responses protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) per spec §8 - Deterministic SHA-256-based session ID derivation (matching Python SDK: `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` → 63-char hex) - `ResponsesActivitySource` and `ResponsesTracingConstants` were made **internal** (breaking change for users who referenced them for custom spans) - Startup configuration logging via `ResponsesStartupLogger` (storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, event stream TTL) - Chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints - Isolation key presence logging (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`) on all endpoint logs (values are never logged) - Foundry storage URL masking in diagnostic logs ## Gaps Found - The README does not document the `x-agent-session-id` response header that clients will now receive on every protocol endpoint — users building client code against this server need to know to expect and optionally consume this header - The README troubleshooting/logging section still implies `ResponsesActivitySource` is publicly usable ("emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source") but does not note it is now internal — any handler author who was referencing this type directly will hit a compile error with no guidance - The deterministic session ID derivation (SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"`) is not documented in the README; this is important for operators who need to understand how session affinity works across requests - The startup configuration logging (what the server logs at startup, and which fields are masked for security) is absent from the README troubleshooting section - Chat isolation key enforcement behavior (mismatched key returns 404 indistinguishable from not-found) is not documented in the README Key Concepts or Troubleshooting sections 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit **1a. Add `x-agent-session-id` response header to Key Concepts** After the "### Thread safety" subsection, add a new subsection: ```markdown ### Session ID Response Header Every Responses protocol endpoint — POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems — includes an `x-agent-session-id` response header per the agent server specification §8. The value is the resolved session ID for the request, derived in the following priority order: 1. `agent_session_id` from the POST request body 2. `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable 3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` (63 lowercase hex chars), where the partition hint is extracted from `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` 4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available Clients that need to maintain session affinity across a multi-turn conversation can read this header from the POST response and supply it as `agent_session_id` in subsequent requests. ``` **1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section** Replace: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues. ``` With: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry distributed traces using the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source name. The `ResponsesActivitySource` class is internal to the framework; do not reference it directly in handler code. **Startup logging:** When the host starts, `ResponsesStartupLogger` logs the active configuration at `Information` level: - Storage provider type (e.g., `FoundryStorageProvider` or `InMemoryResponsesProvider`) - Default model - Default fetch history count - Event stream TTL **Endpoint diagnostic logging:** All Responses endpoints log at `Information` level with response ID, conversation ID, isolation key presence (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey` — key values are never logged), and final status. 4xx/5xx responses from inbound request logging are at `Warning` level. **Foundry storage logs:** Outbound Foundry storage API calls are logged with method, masked URI (everything before `/storage` is redacted; query parameters are stripped except `api-version`), status code, and correlation headers. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to surface these messages. ``` **1c. Add isolation key enforcement to Troubleshooting / Common errors** After the existing 404 Not Found bullet, add: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (isolation mismatch): If a response was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for that response must include the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns 404 intentionally — this is indistinguishable from not-found to protect tenant isolation. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are needed for this documentation update — the changes are prose-only additions to existing sections. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The additions above slot into existing subsections. No structural changes are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — verify the README diff shows only the prose additions above (no snippet regions should have changed) 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24601560857/agentic_workflow) · ● 343.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58327","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-18T09:21:45Z","updated_at":"2026-04-18T09:21:45Z"}

Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section** Replace: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues. ``` With: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry distributed traces using the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source name. The `ResponsesActivitySource` class is internal to the framework; do not reference it directly in handler code. **Startup logging:** When the host starts, `ResponsesStartupLogger` logs the active configuration at `Information` level: - Storage provider type (e.g., `FoundryStorageProvider` or `InMemoryResponsesProvider`) - Default model - Default fetch history count - Event stream TTL **Endpoint diagnostic logging:** All Responses endpoints log at `Information` level with response ID, conversation ID, isolation key presence (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey` — key values are never logged), and final status. 4xx/5xx responses from inbound request logging are at `Warning` level. **Foundry storage logs:** Outbound Foundry storage API calls are logged with method, masked URI (everything before `/storage` is redacted; query parameters are stripped except `api-version`), status code, and correlation headers. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to surface these messages. ``` **1c. Add isolation key enforcement to Troubleshooting / Common errors** After the existing 404 Not Found bullet, add: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (isolation mismatch): If a response was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for that response must include the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns 404 intentionally — this is indistinguishable from not-found to protect tenant isolation. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are needed for this documentation update — the changes are prose-only additions to existing sections. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The additions above slot into existing subsections. No structural changes are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — verify the README diff shows only the prose additions above (no snippet regions should have changed) 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24601560857/agentic_workflow) · ● 343.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58327","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-18T09:21:45Z","updated_at":"2026-04-18T09:21:45Z"}
{"color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a non-management package","id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ3ODYw","name":"Client"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` README introduced by PR #58314, including missing documentation for the `x-agent-session-id` response header, the internalization of `ResponsesActivitySource`, deterministic session ID derivation, startup configuration logging, and isolation key enforcement behavior.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.1)
**Affected API:** README documentation — Key Concepts, Troubleshooting/Logging sections
**Scenarios:**
- Clients building against the server need to know the `x-agent-session-id` header is returned on all Responses protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, InputItems)
- Handler authors who referenced `ResponsesActivitySource` directly will get compile errors — README still implies public usability
- Operators need to understand deterministic SHA-256 session ID derivation for session affinity
- Startup configuration logging fields are undocumented in the Troubleshooting section
- Isolation key enforcement returning 404 on mismatch is undocumented and confusing

**Root ask:** Update the README with prose additions for session ID header, activity source internalization note, startup logging, endpoint diagnostic logging, and isolation key 404 behavior.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation issue, not a code defect. The issue body contains a detailed Implementation Guide with specific file edits to `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58314 to confirm all behavioral changes are accurately described in the issue
2. Apply the prose additions to `README.md` as outlined in the Implementation Guide (sections 1a, 1b, 1c)
3. Verify README structure is intact after edits (all required sections present)
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to confirm no snippet regions changed
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — The issue concerns SDK documentation for a client/server framework package (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`), not a management or provisioning SDK
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — The package lives in `sdk/agentserver/` which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS
- **Confidence:** High — Package name is explicitly stated in the issue body, unambiguously maps to a single service directory and service label
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — the issue's predicted service label "AI Agents" matches this entry
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko, @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; `@mention`ed ServiceOwners via routing comment. "needs-team-attention" was not added because this is a bot-filed issue (not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; no management or other service entries matched "AI Agents". The `%AI Agents` entry at line 85 was the first and only match.
</details>
